title: lycianthes laevis subsp. bigeminata
intro: Lycianthes laevis subsp. bigeminata, also known as the Chilean potato bush, is a type of flowering plant native to the Andes mountains in South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Lycianthes laevis subsp. bigeminata is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
